title: Estate Taxes: What Most Families Don't Need to Worry About
summary: Estate tax is a source of real anxiety for many families, even though it applies to a relatively small share of estates.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Estate Taxes: What Most Families Don't Need to Worry About | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Estate tax is a source of real anxiety for many families, even though it applies to a relatively small share of estates.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Federal estate tax only applies above a very high exemption threshold, affecting a small percentage of estates.
Some states have their own, sometimes lower, estate or inheritance tax thresholds.
Most families dealing with an average estate won't owe federal estate tax at all.

paragraphs: The federal estate tax exemption is quite high, meaning the large majority of estates fall well below the threshold and owe no federal estate tax whatsoever — a fact that often isn't well understood, causing unnecessary worry.

Some states, however, have their own estate or inheritance taxes with lower thresholds than the federal exemption, so it's worth checking state-specific rules even if federal estate tax clearly isn't a concern.

nextSteps: Check the current federal estate tax exemption amount, which changes periodically.
Check whether your state has a separate estate or inheritance tax.
Consult a tax professional if the estate's value is genuinely near any relevant threshold.
disclaimer: General information, not tax advice — thresholds change over time, and a tax professional can confirm current figures.
